Burnt (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $13,651,946Details
International Box Office $23,114,347
Worldwide Box Office $36,766,293
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,278,434
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $879,082
Total $3,157,516
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Chef Adam Jones had it all—and lost it. A two-star Michelin rockstar with the bad habits to match, the former enfant terrible of the Paris restaurant scene did everything different every time out, and only ever cared about the thrill of creating explosions of taste. To land his own kitchen and that third elusive Michelin star though, he’ll need the best of the best on his side, including the beautiful Helene.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$5,002,341 (36.6% of total gross)
Legs:2.73 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:37.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$20,000,000 (worldwide box office is 1.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,003 opening theaters/3,003 max. theaters, 2.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $19,366,529

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: October 30th, 2015 (Wide) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: October 22nd, 2015 (Wide) (Slovenia)
October 22nd, 2015 (Wide) (Croatia)
October 22nd, 2015 (Wide) (Czech Republic)
October 22nd, 2015 (Wide) (Greece)
October 22nd, 2015 (Wide) (Portugal)
Video Release: January 15th, 2016 by Anchor Bay Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language throughout.
(Rating bulletin 2396 (Cert #50048), 10/21/2015)
Running Time: 100 minutes
Plot point: Addiction, Anger Management Issues, Boss From Hell, Food, Life on the Outside, Misanthrope, Professional Rivalry, Rehab / Former Addict, Restaurants

Source material: Inspired by a True Story
Subgenre: Comedy Drama

Genre:Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Chef Adam Jones had it all—and lost it. A two-star Michelin rockstar with the bad habits to match, the former enfant terrible of the Paris restaurant scene did everything different every time out, and only ever cared about the thrill of creating explosions of taste. To land his own kitchen and that third elusive Michelin star though, he’ll need the best of the best on his side, including the beautiful Helene.

Opening Weekend:$5,002,341 (36.6% of total gross)
